doc: Per (backend, profile) cold-start TAX budget in milliseconds. startup_tax_ms = (molt_cold_total - molt_warm_total) * 1000 is the fixed startup cost molt pays on the cold path that the warm steady-state does not. A cell whose startup_tax_ms EXCEEDS budget_ms is FAIL_COLD_BUDGET (a startup regression, routed to the cold-start lane -- NOT an execution-engine red). v0 = the measured baseline from the authoritative origin/main board (council ruling A: 'v0 = current measured baseline; near-term = no regression from baseline'). The budget is a CEILING on the tax, NOT on cold_total. release-output native Y1 target: startup_tax < 100ms AND cold_total <= CPython cold_total. NB: the board's startup_tax is a single first-run (page-cold + often a freshly-built binary's one-time codesign); the realistic same-path tax is ~18ms (see docs/perf/COLD_START.md) -- the v0 budget bounds the FIRST-RUN tax so the gate does not regress, while the cold-start decomposition localizes the size-driven page-in lever to attack.

native/release-fast.basis: v0 = measured max 341ms + ~10% headroom, rounded. All current native cells pass (no FAIL_COLD_BUDGET at v0, per 'no regression from baseline'). Ratchet DOWN as binary-page-in shrinks (the #62 lever).

native/release-output.basis: not board-measured yet (the v0 board is release-fast). release-output is the shipped artifact; council Y1 target is startup_tax < 100ms. The cold-start decomposition (docs/perf/COLD_START.md) measured release-output's realistic same-path tax at ~18ms; seed budget_ms from a release-output board run, then ratchet toward 100ms.

llvm/release-fast.basis: v0 = measured max 307ms + ~10% headroom, rounded. All current llvm cells pass at v0.
